featured.title: Charter Communications data breach affects 4.9 million accounts
featured.summary: The ShinyHunters extortion gang stole personal information from 4.9 million accounts after hacking the U.S. telecom giant Charter Communications in early April, according to data breach notification service Have I Been Pwned. [...]
featured.suggestedTitle: Charter Communications Data Breach Exposes 4.9 Million Accounts
featured.suggestedSummary: The ShinyHunters extortion gang has breached Charter Communications, compromising personal data of 4.9 million accounts. The breach involves sensitive customer information, potentially leading to identity theft and financial fraud. Security teams should monitor for suspicious activities and strengthen data protection measures to mitigate risks associated with this breach.

featured.title: ChatGPhish Vulnerability Turns ChatGPT Web Summaries Into a Phishing Surface
featured.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a vulnerability in OpenAI ChatGPT that leverages the artificial intelligence (AI) assistant's implicit trust in Markdown links and images to trigger prompt injections and open the door to phishing attacks.

The technique has been codenamed ChatGPhish by Permiso Security.

"The chatgpt.com response renderer trusts Markdown links and Markdown
featured.suggestedTitle: AI Exploits: ChatGPhish and LLM Agent Post-Exploitation
featured.suggestedSummary: Cybersecurity researchers have identified new vulnerabilities in OpenAI's ChatGPT, including a phishing surface known as ChatGPhish, which exploits web summaries. Additionally, threat actors are leveraging large language model (LLM) agents for post-exploitation tasks following the Marimo CVE-2026-39987 exploit. These developments highlight the evolving threat landscape where AI is being used to enhance cyberattacks. Security teams should update defenses to address these novel attack vectors.

featured.title: Dutch govt disrupts malware botnet with 17 million infected devices
featured.summary: Dutch authorities have taken offline a massive botnet of 17 million devices and seized more than 200 servers at a local provider that supported the operation. [...]
featured.reason: Botnet disruption involving millions of devices
featured.suggestedTitle: Dutch Authorities Dismantle Massive Botnet of 17 Million Devices
featured.suggestedSummary: Dutch authorities have successfully taken down a botnet comprising 17 million infected devices, associated with a Russia-based residential proxy network. This operation involved seizing over 200 servers used for command and control. The disruption of such a large botnet reduces the risk of DDoS attacks and other malicious activities. Organizations should remain vigilant and ensure their devices are not part of such networks by maintaining up-to-date security patches and monitoring network traffic.

quickNews.title: ChatGPT share links abused to host fake outage pages to deliver malware
quickNews.summary: Threat actors are abusing ChatGPT's content-sharing feature to display fake OpenAI outage pages that direct users to download malware disguised as the ChatGPT desktop application. [...]

quickNews.title: New Russian-Linked GREYVIBE Targets Ukraine with AI-Powered Cyberattacks
quickNews.summary:
A previously undocumented threat actor dubbed GREYVIBE has been attributed to ongoing and persistent attacks targeting Ukraine and Ukraine-related entities since at least August 2025.

quickNews.title: Google Chrome adds session cookie theft protection for all users
quickNews.summary: Google says the Chrome Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) security feature is now generally available and is rolling out to all users to prevent account takeovers. [...]
